key_highlights: SpaceX's historic $75 billion IPO surged 19% on its first trading day, marking the largest stock debut ever and signaling strong investor appetite for space and tech IPOs.

US and Iran appear close to a deal, with former President Trump claiming a signing tomorrow and the reopening of the Hormuz Strait, driving a 400-point Dow rally on hopes of de-escalation and lower oil prices.

AI continues to dominate headlines: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang predicts the next $1 trillion company will be in AI, Google Search gets an AI makeover, and the UK announces a major AI infrastructure push.

Geopolitical tensions remain: US killed a Tren de Aragua leader, China rebukes US over military labeling of firms, and the EU reviews the Paramount-Warner merger over Middle Eastern wealth fund backing.
Inflation concerns persist: Trump comments on rising CPI, fuel tax changes hit six states, and ECB's Nagel warns prices may stay higher even if conflict ends.
Crypto markets are buzzing: BitMine reports $9.6B in crypto holdings, Ripple pushes XRP/RLUSD for AI agent payments, and institutional money flows into XRP ETFs while fleeing Bitcoin and Ethereum.
Gold miners are behaving like meme stocks, and the bond market sees CLO ETFs booming on higher rates.
Labor market shifts: Recent graduates report AI taking entry-level jobs, while SpaceX's IPO created millionaires among former employees and welders.

sectors.trend: Strong bullish momentum driven by SpaceX's record IPO and AI infrastructure investments globally. Nvidia's CEO sees AI as the next trillion-dollar opportunity. Google's AI makeover and UK's AI push underscore the theme.
sectors.implications: Investors should overweight AI-related hardware, software, and infrastructure plays. SpaceX's success may lift other space and tech IPOs. However, high valuations warrant selective entry.

sectors.trend: Oil prices face downward pressure from potential Iran deal and Hormuz reopening, but ECB warns prices may stay elevated. Gold miners are volatile, moving like meme stocks.
sectors.implications: Energy stocks may see short-term weakness if deal is signed. Gold miners' erratic behavior suggests caution. Oil services and tanker stocks could be impacted by Hormuz reopening.

sectors.trend: Institutional rotation from Bitcoin/Ethereum into XRP ETFs is notable. BitMine's massive crypto treasury and Ripple's push for AI agent payments signal growing utility. BlackRock's potential game-changer looms.
sectors.implications: XRP and related tokens may outperform in the near term. Tokenized assets (CLO funds on Solana) are gaining traction. Investors should monitor regulatory clarity and ETF flows.

sectors.trend: Paramount-Skydance acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery gets DOJ approval but faces EU review over Middle Eastern wealth funds. Korean media giant JTBC defaults, signaling stress in legacy media.
sectors.implications: Consolidation in media continues, but regulatory hurdles remain. Investors should favor streaming and content companies with strong IP. Korean media default is a warning for emerging market media bonds.

sectors.trend: Cytokinetics launches MYQORZO in Germany for HCM. Bristol Myers Squibb dividend safety questioned. Ensign Group acquires skilled nursing facility. Hinge Health holds analyst day.
sectors.implications: Biotech with approved products and clear catalysts are attractive. Dividend safety in pharma needs scrutiny given inflation. Healthcare services (skilled nursing) benefit from aging demographics.

sectors.trend: AECOM wins DHS modernization contract. Stantec and Jacobs win Melbourne water contract. AeroVironment expands production in Ohio. Broadridge and Jack Henry expand buybacks.
sectors.implications: Government infrastructure spending and defense contracts provide steady revenue. Water and cybersecurity infrastructure are growth areas. Buyback expansions signal management confidence.

sectors.trend: China urges motorcycle exporters to curb price wars. Geely to shut units, focus on HK-listed arm. Designer home décor supplier files Chapter 11. Brazil's World Cup grilling challenged by beef prices.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary faces margin pressure from inflation and price wars. Home décor bankruptcy signals weakness in housing-related spending. Auto sector restructuring in China.

sectors.trend: Fed and BOE stay guarded after 100 days of Iran war. CLO ETFs boom on higher rates and private debt woes. Bond investors weigh BND vs MUB. Fuel tax changes hit six states.
sectors.implications: Rates likely to remain higher for longer. CLO ETFs offer yield but carry credit risk. Municipal bonds may benefit from tax-exempt status in a rising tax environment. Short-duration bonds preferred.

key_themes: SpaceX IPO mania and its ripple effects on tech valuations and retail investor participation
AI infrastructure buildout globally (US, UK, China) and its impact on compute, energy, and labor
Geopolitical de-escalation (Iran deal) vs. persistent inflation and central bank hawkishness
Crypto institutional rotation from Bitcoin/Ethereum to XRP and tokenized assets
Media consolidation with regulatory scrutiny (Paramount-Warner, EU review)
Labor market disruption from AI replacing entry-level jobs
Gold miners' unusual volatility and disconnect from gold prices
Private markets fighting back against public scrutiny
Healthcare innovation in rare diseases and skilled nursing
Infrastructure spending driven by government contracts and climate adaptation

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Geopolitical escalation despite Iran deal hopes
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify across regions and sectors; hold energy and defense positions as hedges; monitor Hormuz shipping insurance rates.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Persistent inflation and central bank tightening
risk_assessment.mitigation: Favor short-duration bonds, TIPS, and inflation-protected securities; reduce exposure to rate-sensitive sectors like real estate and utilities; overweight commodities and value stocks.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: AI-driven labor displacement and social unrest
risk_assessment.mitigation: Invest in AI beneficiaries (Nvidia, Palantir) but also in retraining and education stocks; monitor consumer sentiment and political responses.
Limit crypto exposure to liquid, regulated ETFs; avoid leveraged tokens; monitor SEC and CFTC actions on XRP and stablecoins.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Media sector debt defaults and consolidation risks
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid high-yield media bonds; focus on companies with strong balance sheets and streaming growth; monitor EU regulatory decisions on mergers.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Gold miner volatility and disconnect from gold
risk_assessment.mitigation: Prefer gold ETFs over miners; use options to hedge miner positions; focus on low-cost producers with strong margins.
Reduce exposure to Chinese tech ADRs; favor US semiconductor and AI companies with domestic supply chains; monitor export controls.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy SpaceX on any post-IPO pullback or via pre-IPO funds
opportunities.rationale: SpaceX's 19% first-day gain and $75B IPO size indicate strong demand. The company's dominance in space launch and Starlink provides long-term growth. Retail investors who missed the IPO can look for secondary market opportunities.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate Nvidia (NVDA) and other AI infrastructure plays
opportunities.rationale: Nvidia CEO's prediction of the next $1 trillion AI company and global AI buildout (UK, US) support sustained demand for GPUs and data center infrastructure. Google's AI makeover adds to the theme.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in XRP ETFs and tokenized asset platforms
opportunities.rationale: Institutional rotation into XRP ETFs, Ripple's push for AI agent payments, and tokenized CLO funds on Solana signal growing utility. BitMine's massive crypto treasury adds credibility.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy AECOM (ACM) and Stantec (STN) on infrastructure contract wins
opportunities.rationale: DHS and Melbourne water contracts provide multi-year revenue visibility. Government infrastructure spending is bipartisan and recession-resistant.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider Cytokinetics (CYTK) on product launch momentum
opportunities.rationale: MYQORZO launch in Germany for obstructive HCM addresses a large unmet need. Approval in additional markets could drive revenue growth.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy municipal bonds (MUB) for tax-exempt income
opportunities.rationale: With fuel tax increases and potential for higher taxes, muni bonds offer attractive after-tax yields. BND vs MUB analysis favors munis in a rising rate environment.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to gold miners and switch to gold ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: Gold miners are moving like meme stocks, indicating speculative froth. Gold ETFs (GLD, IAU) provide safer exposure to the metal without operational risks.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against oil price volatility with put spreads on energy stocks
defensive_moves.rationale: Iran deal could cause sharp oil price declines, hurting energy stocks. Use put spreads on XLE or OIH to protect against downside while maintaining upside if deal fails.
Fed and BOE remain guarded, and ECB warns of persistent inflation. Short-duration bonds (SHY, BSV) reduce interest rate risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid Chinese tech ADRs amid escalating tensions
defensive_moves.rationale: China's dissatisfaction with US moves and US military labeling of Chinese firms increase regulatory and delisting risks. Focus on US-listed AI and semiconductor companies.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Sell or reduce positions in legacy media and Korean media bonds
defensive_moves.rationale: JTBC default and EU scrutiny of Paramount-Warner merger indicate stress. Avoid high-yield media debt and focus on streaming leaders with strong IP.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets are likely to remain volatile but with a positive bias driven by Iran deal hopes and SpaceX's IPO success. The Dow's 400-point rally suggests risk-on sentiment. However, inflation data and central bank comments could trigger pullbacks. AI and crypto sectors may continue to outperform, while energy and gold miners face headwinds. The World Cup and measles outbreak are non-market risks to monitor.
market_outlook.long_term: The AI revolution and space economy are structural growth drivers. Infrastructure spending and defense contracts provide steady income. Inflation may stay elevated, favoring value and commodity-linked assets. Geopolitical risks (Iran, China-US) remain but could de-escalate. Crypto adoption by institutions and tokenization of real-world assets are transformative trends. Labor market disruption from AI will require policy responses but also create investment opportunities in automation and retraining.
key_catalysts: Iran deal signing and Hormuz reopening (June 14?)
Fed and BOE policy decisions in coming weeks
SpaceX secondary trading and potential inclusion in indices
Nvidia earnings and AI capex guidance from hyperscalers
XRP ETF inflows and regulatory clarity
US CPI and PCE data releases
World Cup-related economic impact on host nations
China stimulus measures and tech crackdown updates
watch_list: Oil prices (WTI, Brent) and Hormuz shipping insurance rates
Gold price vs. gold miner ETF (GDX) divergence
XRP price and ETF flow data
SpaceX stock price and volume in secondary markets
US dollar index (DXY) and emerging market currencies
Bitcoin dominance vs. altcoin market cap
AI job displacement headlines and policy responses
Measles outbreak spread and travel restrictions
Korean media bond spreads and default contagion
